en: Now in the twelfth month, which is the month of Adar, on the thirteenth day of the same, when the king's command and edict were about to be carried out, on the very day when the enemies of the Jews hoped to gain the mastery over them, the reverse occurred: the Jews gained mastery over those who hated them.
zh: 十二月，就是亞達月，十三日，王的命令和諭旨快要執行的時候，就是猶大人的仇敵盼望轄制猶大人的日子，反成了猶大人轄制恨他們的人的日子。

en: The Jews gathered in their cities throughout all the provinces of King Ahasuerus to lay hands on those who sought their harm. And no one could stand against them, for the fear of them had fallen on all peoples.
zh: 猶大人在亞哈隨魯王各省的城裡聚集起來，要下手攻擊那些想謀害他們的人；沒有人能抵擋他們，因為各族的人都懼怕他們。

en: All the officials of the provinces and the satraps and the governors and the royal agents also helped the Jews, for the fear of Mordecai had fallen on them.
zh: 各省的官長、總督、省長和辦理王事務的人，因為懼怕末底改，就都支持猶大人。

en: For Mordecai was great in the king's house, and his fame spread throughout all the provinces, for the man Mordecai grew more and more powerful.
zh: 末底改在朝中的確非常尊大，他的聲譽傳遍各省；末底改這人的權力越來越大。

en: The Jews struck all their enemies with the sword, killing and destroying them, and did as they pleased to those who hated them.
zh: 這樣，猶大人擊殺他們所有的仇敵，他們用刀盡量擊殺，隨意對待他們的仇人。

en: In Susa the citadel itself the Jews killed and destroyed 500 men,
zh: 在書珊城裡，猶大人就殺滅了五百人。

en: and also killed Parshandatha and Dalphon and Aspatha
zh: 他們又殺了巴珊大他、達分、亞斯帕他、

en: and Poratha and Adalia and Aridatha
zh: 破拉他、亞大利雅、亞利大他、

en: and Parmashta and Arisai and Aridai and Vaizatha,
zh: 帕瑪斯他、亞利賽、亞利代和瓦耶撒他，

en: the ten sons of Haman the son of Hammedatha, the enemy of the Jews, but they laid no hand on the plunder.
zh: 這十個人就是哈米大他的孫子、哈曼的兒子，猶大人的敵人；至於財物，猶大人卻沒有下手掠奪。

en: That very day the number of those killed in Susa the citadel was reported to the king.
zh: 當日在書珊城被殺的人的數目，呈到王面前。

en: And the king said to Queen Esther, "In Susa the citadel the Jews have killed and destroyed 500 men and also the ten sons of Haman. What then have they done in the rest of the king's provinces! Now what is your wish? It shall be granted you. And what further is your request? It shall be fulfilled."
zh: 王問王后以斯帖：“猶大人在書珊城殺滅了五百人，又殺了哈曼的十個兒子；在王其他省內，他們怎樣行呢！現在你要甚麼，我必賜給你，你還求甚麼，也必給你成全。”

en: And Esther said, "If it please the king, let the Jews who are in Susa be allowed tomorrow also to do according to this day's edict. And let the ten sons of Haman be hanged on the gallows."
zh: 以斯帖回答：“王若是同意，求你恩准書珊的猶大人，明日也照著今日的諭旨行事，把哈曼的十個兒子掛在木架上。”

en: So the king commanded this to be done. A decree was issued in Susa, and the ten sons of Haman were hanged.
zh: 王就下令這樣行；諭旨傳遍書珊，人就把哈曼的十個兒子掛在木架上。

en: The Jews who were in Susa gathered also on the fourteenth day of the month of Adar and they killed 300 men in Susa, but they laid no hands on the plunder.
zh: 亞達月十四日，在書珊的猶大人又聚集起來，在書珊殺了三百人，卻沒有下手掠奪他們的財物。

en: Now the rest of the Jews who were in the king's provinces also gathered to defend their lives, and got relief from their enemies and killed 75,000 of those who hated them, but they laid no hands on the plunder.
zh: 在王各省其餘的猶大人，也都聚集起來，保護自己的性命，向他們的仇敵報復，殺了恨他們的人共七萬五千；至於財物，他們卻沒有下手掠奪。

en: This was on the thirteenth day of the month of Adar, and on the fourteenth day they rested and made that a day of feasting and gladness.
zh: 這是在亞達月十三日的事，十四日他們得享安寧，以這日為設宴歡樂的日子。

en: But the Jews who were in Susa gathered on the thirteenth day and on the fourteenth, and rested on the fifteenth day, making that a day of feasting and gladness.
zh: 但在書珊的猶大人在十三、十四日聚集起來殺敵，他們就在十五日休息，以這日為設宴歡樂的日子。

en: Therefore the Jews of the villages, who live in the rural towns, hold the fourteenth day of the month of Adar as a day for gladness and feasting, as a holiday, and as a day on which they send gifts of food to one another.
zh: 因此鄉村的猶大人，就是住在沒有城牆的村鎮的，都以亞達月十四日為歡樂飲宴的吉日，互送禮物。

en: And Mordecai recorded these things and sent letters to all the Jews who were in all the provinces of King Ahasuerus, both near and far,
zh: 末底改把這些事記錄下來，並且送文書給亞哈隨魯各省遠近所有的猶大人，

en: obliging them to keep the fourteenth day of the month Adar and also the fifteenth day of the same, year by year,
zh: 囑咐他們每年都要守亞達月十四、十五兩日。

en: as the days on which the Jews got relief from their enemies, and as the month that had been turned for them from sorrow into gladness and from mourning into a holiday; that they should make them days of feasting and gladness, days for sending gifts of food to one another and gifts to the poor.
zh: 以這月的兩日，為猶大人脫離仇敵，得享安寧的日子，是轉憂為喜，轉哀為樂的吉日，叫他們在這兩日飲宴歡樂，大家互贈禮物，也賙濟窮人。

en: So the Jews accepted what they had started to do, and what Mordecai had written to them.
zh: 於是，猶大人承諾要守他們起初所守的，也承諾要遵守末底改寫給他們的。

en: For Haman the Agagite, the son of Hammedatha, the enemy of all the Jews, had plotted against the Jews to destroy them, and had cast Pur (that is, cast lots), to crush and to destroy them.
zh: 原本猶大人的敵人，亞甲族哈米大他的兒子哈曼曾經設謀陷害猶大人，要消滅他們；他弄卜“普珥”，就是抽籤，為要打擊和消滅猶大人。

en: But when it came before the king, he gave orders in writing that his evil plan that he had devised against the Jews should return on his own head, and that he and his sons should be hanged on the gallows.
zh: 但這事呈到王面前以後，王使用文書降旨吩咐，使哈曼計謀的惡事，就是他設謀陷害猶大人的事歸到他自己的頭上，又使人把他和他的眾子掛在木架上。

en: Therefore they called these days Purim, after the term Pur. Therefore, because of all that was written in this letter, and of what they had faced in this matter, and of what had happened to them,
zh: 因此，猶大人照著“普珥”的名字，稱這兩日為普珥節；所以他們因這信上的話，又因他們所看見和所遭遇的事，

en: the Jews firmly obligated themselves and their offspring and all who joined them, that without fail they would keep these two days according to what was written and at the time appointed every year,
zh: 猶大人就立了定例，承諾他們自己和他們的後裔，以及所有與他們聯合的人，每年都要按著所寫的和指定的兩日守這節日，永不可廢止。

en: that these days should be remembered and kept throughout every generation, in every clan, province, and city, and that these days of Purim should never fall into disuse among the Jews, nor should the commemoration of these days cease among their descendants.
zh: 使人在各省各城，家家戶戶，世世代代，都記念、遵守這兩日，使這普珥節的日子在猶大人中，永不廢止，在他們的後裔中也不斷被記念。

en: Then Queen Esther, the daughter of Abihail, and Mordecai the Jew gave full written authority, confirming this second letter about Purim.
zh: 亞比孩的女兒王后以斯帖和猶大人末底改以全權寫了這第二封信，囑咐猶大人守這普珥節。

en: Letters were sent to all the Jews, to the 127 provinces of the kingdom of Ahasuerus, in words of peace and truth,
zh: 他用和平和誠實的話，把文書送給亞哈隨魯國中一百二十七省所有的猶大人，

en: that these days of Purim should be observed at their appointed seasons, as Mordecai the Jew and Queen Esther obligated them, and as they had obligated themselves and their offspring, with regard to their fasts and their lamenting.
zh: 勸他們按著規定的時期，照著猶大人末底改和王后以斯帖所囑咐的，也照著他們在禁食和哀求的時候為自己與後裔所承諾的，守這普珥節。

en: The command of Queen Esther confirmed these practices of Purim, and it was recorded in writing.
zh: 以斯帖的命令確定了普珥節；這命令也記錄在史記上。
